pi_items.source: export_result.sh explicitly searches the Maildir for the TARGET_FOLDER='.Champion-Candidate', which corresponds to a user-created 'Champion-Candidate' folder.
verifier.py retrieves min_emails from metadata and checks if the moved emails are >= 2.
verifier.py explicitly extracts expected_keywords from metadata to verify the outreach email's subject.

privileged_info_summary: The environment uses real email data from the SpamAssassin Public Corpus. A python script execution confirmed that out of the 50 seeded ham emails, exactly 7 external senders meet the condition of having sent >= 2 emails (e.g. martin@srv0.ems.ed.ac.uk and cout@eircom.net have 3). The folder name is strictly verified as 'Champion-Candidate' and the outreach email must be sent to the consistent sender with at least one matching subject keyword ('champion', 'community', 'invite', 'program').
